teaching_arc.entry_point: Welcome back after optimization and L'Hôpital, tangent lines from Topic 9 become local linear models, then root-finding iterations.
teaching_arc.progression: Linear approximation theorem → Newton formula and derivation → visual lab → worked estimates (e^x, √x) → misconception traps → Newton examples and failure modes → Session 4 recap → integration teaser.
teaching_arc.destination: Students build L(x), run Newton steps, diagnose bad anchors/starting points, and see how Topics 15–21 close before antiderivatives.

topic_goal: Teach linear approximations and Newton's method as tangent-line applications that close Session 4 and preview integration.
topic_takeaways: State and use L(x)=f(a)+f'(a)(x-a) for local estimates
Derive and apply x_{n+1}=x_n-f(x_n)/f'(x_n) for root-finding
Recognize linear approx is only accurate near a
Diagnose Newton failures: f'(x_n)=0, bad x_0, vertical tangent at root
Summarize Session 4 Topics 15–21 and preview Topic 22 antiderivatives
style_notes: Welcome back, do not re-introduce Arian from scratch.
Callback Topic 9 tangent line; Topics 19 optimization and 20 L'Hôpital as immediate predecessors.
theorem_box on s01 for L(x); secant_tangent shrinking h → tangent as best linear approx.
flex_plot: unique curve + dashed tangent per slide, never repeat identical plot specs.
s04 visual_lab: demo tab A on YouTube (~30s); all three tabs on site.
misconception_compare s07: far-from-a linear approx AND bad Newton x₀.
pause_and_reveal on practice and challenge slides, real think beats, not decorative.
s16: six bullets Topics 15–21 + Topic 22 Antiderivatives teaser by name.
transition_style: Tangent line → local estimate → set L(x)=0 for Newton → convergence caveats → Session 4 finale → integration arc.

slides.narration_seed: Welcome back to Arian University. Topic 19 optimization and Topic 20 L'Hôpital are behind us, Topic 21 closes Session 4. We reuse the tangent line from Topic 9: first as a local linear estimate L(x), then as the engine for Newton's root-finding. Next session starts integration with antiderivatives.
must_cover: Callbacks Topics 19–20 and Topic 9 tangent line
slides.transition_from_previous: Open after L'Hôpital, derivatives now estimate values and solve equations.
slides.transition_to_next: Roadmap on learning objectives, Topics 1–20 done, Topic 22 next.
slides.explanation_style: Frame Session 4 finale and dual goals: linear approx + Newton.
slides.story_hint: Tangent line is the thread from Topic 9 through Topic 21.
slides.delivery_goal: Students know this topic closes derivative applications and previews integration.

slides.narration_seed: On the roadmap, Topics 1 through 20 are complete. Topic 21 is linear approximations and Newton's method. Topic 22 antiderivatives is next in Session 5. By the end you'll estimate with L(x), iterate Newton steps, and spot when tangent models break down.
slides.transition_from_previous: From title hook into structured objectives and roadmap.
slides.transition_to_next: theorem_box for L(x) on the next slide.
slides.delivery_goal: Students see where Topic 21 sits and what they will master.

slide_ref.source_title: Linear Approximation: The Tangent Line Formula

slides.narration_seed: theorem_box: L(x)=f(a)+f'(a)(x-a). Near a, the tangent is the best linear stand-in for f. Watch secant_tangent, shrink h and the secant slope approaches f'(a). Callback Topic 9: same tangent line you derived from the limit definition.
must_cover: Tangent is best first-order approx near a
slides.transition_to_next: Newton uses the same tangent, set L(x)=0.
slides.explanation_style: Formal L(x) plus secant shrinking to tangent.
slides.story_hint: Best linear match near a, not a global replacement.
slides.delivery_goal: Students state L(x) and connect to Topic 9 tangent line.

slides.narration_seed: Newton's update: x_{n+1}=x_n-f(x_n)/f'(x_n). Foggy-trail analogy: lay a stick along the slope, walk to the river crossing, repeat. On the flex_plot, the vertical leg is f(x_n) and the horizontal correction is f(x_n)/f'(x_n).
slides.transition_from_previous: Same tangent template, now hunt roots instead of estimates.
slides.transition_to_next: Derive the formula from tangent line algebra.
slides.delivery_goal: Students read Newton formula and its triangle geometry.

slides.narration_seed: Reveal math_solution_steps: tangent equation, set y=0, solve for x. Five algebra steps land on x_{n+1}=x_n-f/f'. flex_plot shows solid curve and dashed tangent hitting the axis, algebra matches the picture.
slides.transition_from_previous: Prove the formula students just saw.
slides.transition_to_next: visual_lab to explore linear approx and Newton starts.
slides.story_hint: Linear approx equation with y=0 is Newton.
slides.delivery_goal: Students derive Newton step from tangent intercept.

slide_ref.source_title: Lab: Linear Approx & Newton Starting Points

slides.narration_seed: visual_lab three tabs: e^x at 0 with L(x)=1+x, √x at 4, Newton on x²−2 with different x₀. On video demo tab A, drag probe near 0.1 then far to 2 and watch error grow. Pause: will x₀=−1 converge?
slides.transition_to_next: Worked linear approx e^0.1 with steps.
likely_student_confusion: Using L(x) far from anchor a
slides.explanation_style: Demo one tab; mention others for site.
slides.story_hint: Near-a fit vs Newton starting-point sensitivity.
slides.delivery_goal: Students see three distinct plot tabs and probe behavior.
slides.scene_hint: labExamples params patch plot per tab.

slides.narration_seed: math_solution_steps: f(x)=e^x at a=0 gives L(x)=1+x. L(0.1)=1.1 versus e^0.1≈1.1052, tiny error. Pause: would anchoring at a=2 work? No, only near a. flex_plot zoomed at origin.
slides.explanation_style: Reveal steps; graph confirms near-a fit.

slides.narration_seed: At a=4, f(x)=√x has f(4)=2 and f'(4)=1/4. L(x)=2+(x-4)/4. L(4.2)=2.05 versus √4.2≈2.0494. Pause stretch: estimate √9.1 with a=9.
slides.delivery_goal: Students build L(x) for √x and estimate √4.2.

slide_ref.source_title: Misconceptions: Far from a & Bad Newton Starts

slides.narration_seed: misconception_compare four pairs: L(x) only works near a, using 1+x for e^2 fails; Newton needs f'≠0 and a sensible x₀. flex_plot timeline moves probe from 0.15 to x=2, watch the gap between e^x and its tangent explode.
must_cover: Newton needs good x₀ and f'(x_n)≠0
slides.transition_from_previous: Contrast correct vs tempting wrong uses.
slides.story_hint: Local linear models need local anchors.
slides.delivery_goal: Students name both linear-approx and Newton traps.

slides.narration_seed: Newton on f(x)=x²−5 with x₀=2: x₁=2.25, x₂≈2.2361, four decimals of √5 in two steps. Babylonian form x_{n+1}=(x_n+5/x_n)/2. Pause: plug x₂ back, f≈0.00006.
slides.transition_from_previous: First Newton numeric example after traps.
slides.delivery_goal: Students execute two Newton steps for √5.

slides.narration_seed: Exam-style cubic: x₁=1 gives x₂=1.2, then x₃≈1.1797. Pause before revealing x₂, students compute first. Keep six decimals internally.
slides.delivery_goal: Students reach x₃ for the cubic.

slide_ref.source_title: Misconception: Division by Zero in Newton

slides.narration_seed: f(x)=x³−3x+1 at x₀=1: f'(1)=0 so x₁=1−(−1)/0, horizontal tangent, no intercept. Fix: start at x₀=0. flex_plot dashed horizontal tangent at (1,−1).
scriptedTimeline on flex_plot: probe moves x₀=2 → x₁=1.5 → x₂≈1.4167 → x₃≈1.4142 toward √2. Quadratic convergence, digits double. Mention loan rates and cos x=x as same template.
slides.delivery_goal: Students watch iterates converge on graph.

slides.narration_seed: Optional challenge: f(x)=x^{1/3}, root at 0, x₀=1. Vertical tangent at 0, pause: converge or diverge? Reveal on next slide. Probe timeline 1→−2→4 on flex_plot.
Table: 1, −2, 4, −8 bouncing. f'(r) infinite at root, linear model fails. Tie to linear-approx theme: tangents are local only.
must_cover: link to poor local linear fit
slides.story_hint: When linear approx breaks, Newton breaks too.

slides.narration_seed: Car loan equation, no closed form. x₀=0.01 gives x₁≈0.00823 (0.823% monthly). math_solution_steps on left; practice ladder with pause on right. Skim ladder on video.
slides.delivery_goal: Students see Newton on applied finance equation.

slides.narration_seed: Exam tips: anchor near target for L(x); separate f and f' before dividing; verify roots and estimates; Babylonian √a shortcut; sketch f before picking x₀.

Six bullets recap Topics 15–21: related rates, curve shape, optimization, L'Hôpital, linear approx L(x), Newton iteration. Session 4 complete. Next: Topic 22 Antiderivatives, reverse differentiation. Final pause: L(x)=1+x, estimate e^0.05.
slides.transition_to_next: Close course segment, Session 5 integration arc begins.
slides.explanation_style: Arc recap + forward teaser + final pause.
slides.delivery_goal: Students compress Session 4 arc and know Topic 22 is next.
